nameMeanings.name: Sale
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Sale (Cheshire), or from any of several minor places or topographic features named from Middle English sale, salle ‘sallow, willow’ (Old English s(e)alh). See also Seal , with which this name may often have interchanged.

English: from Middle English sal(e) ‘hall’ (Old English sæl, Old French sale, Old Norse salr), translated by medieval clerks as Latin aula. The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived or worked in a hall, or occupational, for someone who worked at a hall as a servant. This was the name of an Anglo-Norman family who had settled in County Tipperary, Ireland, by the 13th century. The name was Gaelicized as de Sál, hence the Anglicized form Saul which has been widely adopted in Ireland.
Croatian (Šale): probably from a pet form of the personal name Šanto, a Croatized form of Italian Santo .

variants.name: Sallows
meanings: English (Essex and Suffolk): topographic name from Middle English salwe ‘sallow (a species of willow)’ (Old English s(e)alh), with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. Compare Sale .

variants.name: Sales
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Efren, Alberto, Francisco, Manuel, Camilo, Carlos, Fernando, Juan, Juanita, Mario, Ofelia.

English: variant of Sale with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. The Middle English plural form was probably often in variation with the singular form. The surname is uncommon in southwestern England but may derive there from Zeals (Wiltshire), and also from the plural form of Old English sealh ‘willow’; see Seal .
Catalan, Spanish, and French (Occitan): habitational name from any of the places called Sales, like Sales de Llierca (Catalonia), from the plural of   Sala   1. Compare   De Sales  .

variants.name: Salis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ruben, Amparo, Armando, Emilio, Juana, Rodolfo.
Italian (Sardinia): plural form of   Sale   ‘salt’.
French: variant of Salies, a habitational name from a placename Salies in Haute-Garonne and Pyrénées-Atlantiques, ultimately derived from Latin salina ‘salt marsh, salt pan, saltworks’.

variants.name: Seal
meanings: English: either a habitational name from Seal (Kent), Seale (Surrey), or Sele in Upper Beeding (Sussex), all of which probably derive from Old English sele ‘hall, building’ (though the Surrey placename may arise from Old English sēale, dative form of salh ‘willow’), or else a topographic name for someone who lived at a boggy patch (Kentish Middle English and Old English sele) or a hall. Compare Sale 1-2.
English: habitational name from Overseal or Netherseal (Derbyshire), probably from Old English scegel ‘small wood’.

English: topographic name for someone who lived by a willow copse or at a place marked by a willow or willows, from Middle English sele ‘willow’ (Old English sele), in northern England representing Old Norse selja and in southwestern England representing Old English (West Saxon) sealh. The name was probably interchangeable with the synonymous Middle English sale (Old English salh, Old Norse salr), and in some cases the surname may have functioned as a variant of Sale . In southwestern England initial S- was frequently voiced to Z-, as in the Wiltshire placename Zeals (from the plural form of Old English sealh). However, the medieval form of the placename is overwhelmingly in the plural, and this may also survive as Sales .

variants.name: Saile
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Elke, Kurt, Volker.
English: variant of   Sale  .
German: older form of   Seiler  .

variants.name: Sayle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik.
Manx (Isle of Man): variant of   Sale   1.
Possibly a variant or an Americanized form of German   Saile  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Act of Union
historicEvents.description: The Act of Union was a legislative agreement which united England, Scotland, Wales, and Ireland under the name of the United Kingdom on January 1, 1801.
The defeat of Napoleon Bonaparte at Waterloo marks the end of the Napoleonic Wars. Napoleon defeated and exiled to St. Helena.

en.lifeSummary: William Sale was born from 1787 to 1791, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 daughter. He lived in Eccleshall, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom in 1841.

fr.lifeSummary: William Sale est né du 1787 au 1791, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il a vécu à Eccleshall, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni en 1841.

de.lifeSummary: William Sale wurde von 1787 bis 1791 in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. 1841 hat er in Eccleshall, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom gelebt.

it.lifeSummary: William Sale è nato il da 1787 a 1791 nel Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Vissuto a Eccleshall, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito nel 1841.

ja.lifeSummary: William Saleはから1787年まで1791年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアで生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1841年にはイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアエククレシャルに住んでいた。

ko.lifeSummary: William Sale(은)는 1787년 부터 1791년 까지 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1841년도에 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 c클샤에서 살았다.

pt.lifeSummary: William Sale nasceu de 1787 a 1791, em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ele viveu em Eccleshall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom em 1841.

ru.lifeSummary: William Sale родился с 1787 по 1791 в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он жил в местности экклешолл, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в 1841 году.
